# obsidian-llm-wiki (vault-mind MCP) wrapped by supergateway → Streamable HTTP.
# Build args let you pin a ref. The vault is mounted at runtime (see compose).
FROM node:20-slim

RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ARG WIKI_REF=main
WORKDIR /opt
RUN git clone --depth 1 --branch "${WIKI_REF}" https://github.com/2233admin/obsidian-llm-wiki.git wiki

# bundle.js is committed (prebuilt) but esbuild externalizes the runtime deps,
# so they must exist in node_modules. They are all WASM/pure-JS — @electric-sql/
# pglite (WASM), pg, ws, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk — so NO node-gyp/build tools
# are needed. `--omit=optional` skips ws's optional native addon (bufferutil),
# which would otherwise try (and harmlessly fail) to compile on this slim image.
WORKDIR /opt/wiki/mcp-server
RUN npm ci --omit=dev --omit=optional || npm install --omit=dev --omit=optional

# cwd = the vault so vault-mind auto-detects it (also set the env explicitly).
WORKDIR /vault
ENV VAULT_MIND_VAULT_PATH=/vault
EXPOSE 8000

# Launch command per .claude-plugin/mcp.json: `node mcp-server/bundle.js`,
# bridged to Streamable HTTP by supergateway.
CMD ["npx", "-y", "supergateway", \
  "--stdio", "node /opt/wiki/mcp-server/bundle.js", \
  "--outputTransport", "streamableHttp", \
  "--port", "8000"]
